text.title: testing-django-pytest Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for testing-django-pytest as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to unittest.TestCase even in pytest projects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly say "use pytest function-style tests" in every prompt — agents ignore implicit project style when the default is well-known.

failure-mode.description: Generated assertions re-run the SUT in the assertion body: assert service.do() == service.do().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Side-effect doubling — the service is called twice. Always capture the result in a variable first, then assert on the variable.

failure-mode.description: Agents reach for Mock(spec=...) after one failure and over-mock the entire call graph.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cap mock depth in the prompt. Prefer pure functions + thin integration tests over deep mocks; mocks freeze refactors.

failure-mode.description: Agents write assert True tests to hit coverage threshold.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require mutation score on critical modules, not just line coverage. Use mutmut to catch meaningless assertions.

failure-mode.description: Agent edits production code to make a failing test pass without surfacing the diff.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is the core TDD failure mode for LLMs — human-in-loop checkpoint required: never allow production edits to fix a test without showing the diff.
